text: The video shows a serous ovarian cyst on the right side, measuring approximately ten to twelve centimeters, visible during the examination.
Cystectomy is a procedure which needs to be performed extremely meticulously especially for young women who need to preserve their fertility.

The surgeon performs gentle dissection to separate the ovarian cyst from the ovarian cortex, ensuring a bloodless procedure. Using traction and counter traction, the cyst is carefully stripped away from the cortex.
The surgeon considers rupturing the large serous ovarian cyst to debulk it, facilitating easier continuation of the cystectomy procedure.
The surgeon cuts open the ovarian cyst wall into a long ribbon-like structure, following the ribbonization technique to facilitate removal.
The surgeon uses a trocar and scissors through two ports to perform the ribbonization technique, cutting the ovarian cyst wall into a long ribbon and removing it from the peritoneal cavity.
